reference_answer: SYS-B is the Quality Inspection Station (QIS).
R. Kim attended the operator console during the incident window.
SYS-A: 7 events (EV-01, EV-02, EV-03, EV-05, EV-06, EV-09, EV-10); SYS-B: 2 events (EV-08, EV-12); SYS-C: 2 events (EV-13, EV-14); SYS-D: 3 events (EV-04, EV-07, EV-11).
2 named missing sources: the LAB-2026-0422-S3 dryer-inlet moisture analysis (results not yet returned) and the DRY-DL-04 airflow logs covering 14:25–14:38 on 2026-04-22 (lost to buffer overflow).
SYS-C (Maintenance Management System) timestamps are accepted as wall-clock with no drift correction.
Lost (buffer overflow on DRY-DL-04 local datalogger; no recovery).
Sent for analysis on 2026-04-22; results not yet returned as of 2026-04-26 compilation date. Estimated return 2026-04-29.
R. Kim did not originate EV-08 or EV-12; those originated from QIS-OPT-12 automatic flagging.
Unresolved. The packet does not resolve causation; HUM-D-04 is not certified to attribute cause and the named missing evidence (LAB-2026-0422-S3 results and DRY-DL-04 airflow logs) is not available.
No. R. Kim's notes are silent for the 14:50–15:02 interval; the packet states the absence of notes is not affirmative evidence of any specific action.
No. HUM-D-04's certified scope is humidity measurement only; it is not certified for cause attribution.
No. The packet explicitly states it does not assign root cause; that is the function of a separate RCA (in preparation, not part of this packet).
Yes for visual defect classification of pellet output (color uniformity, pellet shape) — within QIS-OPT-12's certified scope. (It is not certified for moisture content estimation.)
SYS-D timestamps are at 1-minute resolution; their precision does not support sub-minute reasoning.

Both packets cover the same incident. This engineering packet (MPP-L4-INC-2026-0422) uses corrected wall-clock times; the regulatory report (MPP-COMP-2026-0427) uses raw timestamps as recorded by each system. Discrepancies are by design, not contradictions.
